question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 (PE1) removes `update-source Loopback0` from its VPNv4 iBGP neighbor
toward R4 (PE2, peering address fc00::4). R2 now sources the IPv6 BGP
session from a link-local or physical interface IPv6 address (not
fc00::2). R4 expects `neighbor fc00::2` and rejects the connection.
VPNv4 session never Established → no VPNv4 routes exchanged → customer
prefix 10.20.0.5/32 (from CE2) doesn't reach VRF CUSTOMER on PE1, and
10.10.0.1/32 (from CE1) doesn't reach PE2. End-to-end VPN dark.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2, PE1), update-source Loopback0 was removed from the VPNv4 iBGP neighbor toward R4 (fc00::4), so R2 sources the IPv6 session from a non-Loopback address instead of fc00::2.
R4 expects neighbor fc00::2 and rejects the connection, so the VPNv4 session never establishes and no VPNv4 routes are exchanged - 10.20.0.5/32 (from CE2) never reaches PE1's VRF CUSTOMER and 10.10.0.1/32 (from CE1) never reaches PE2, leaving the end-to-end VPN dark.
Fix: restore update-source Loopback0 on R2's fc00::4 neighbor.
expected_symptoms_summary:
%SECURITY-SSHD_SYSLOG_PRX-6-INFO_GENERAL reported by R2 (1 instance):
- R2: sshd[5836]: Connection closed by 100.64.0.2 port 60338

BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R2: neighbor fc00::4.
- R4: neighbor fc00::2.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 100
neighbor fc00::4
 no update-source

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 100
neighbor fc00::4
 update-source Loopback0

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 17:03:29.281 UTC: ssh_syslog_proxy[1188]: %SECURITY-SSHD_SYSLOG_PRX-6-INFO_GENERAL : sshd[5836]: Connection closed by 100.64.0.2 port 60338
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 17:03:31.143 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor fc00::4 Down - Neighbor local address configuration changed (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 17:03:31.146 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor fc00::2 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
